note: Estimated API rates, USD per 1M tokens. cw = the model's default cache-write rate (Anthropic 5-minute TTL; GPT-5.6 uses its documented 30-minute-minimum write rate), cw1h = cache-write 1-hour TTL (Anthropic 2x input; absent => falls back to cw). OpenAI models before GPT-5.6 and Google have no additional per-token cache-write fee, so cw=in there; GPT-5.6 and later bill cache writes at 1.25x input. cr = cache-read (~1/10th input). Resolution: models (exact id) -> families (case-insensitive word-boundary substring) -> providers (fallback) -> $0. A turn flagged fast (usage.speed != standard, i.e. Anthropic Fast mode) resolves against the 'fast' section first (exact id then family) and only falls through to the standard tables if no fast rate exists. Cost is computed per turn at the rate in effect on its date, and Anthropic cache-write usage can be split by the 1h/5m token breakdown a consumer records. TWO DISTINCT DATES per rate: 'effective_date' = when this price took effect as far as we know (official change date when announced, else a best-effort floor = first-observed day); 'observed' = the day the number was read (provenance only). When a price CHANGES, ADD a new entry with the real effective_date; do not edit an existing entry's numbers. Models with multiple context tiers carry a `context_pricing` block; consumers select `short` vs `long` per request from the original billed input (`input_tokens_total`), using the provider threshold (`272k` for GPT-5.4/5.5/5.6 family, `200k` for Gemini Pro tiers). Anthropic/Gemini read from the official docs pricing pages; OpenAI (JS-rendered) read via a rendered browser fetch.

rates.source: Anthropic docs (Mythos 5, limited availability) — same rate card as Fable 5
Anthropic docs (Opus 4, deprecated) — exact short-id match; dated build ids may not hit this and fall to the opus family ($5/$25)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.6 alias routes to gpt-5.6-sol; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cache write is 1.25x input)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.6-sol; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cache write is 1.25x input)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.6-terra; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cache write is 1.25x input)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.6-luna; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cache write is 1.25x input)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.5; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long)

OpenAI docs (gpt-5.5-pro; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cached-input rate inferred as 0.1x input because the page omits it) — pinned so 'pro' isn't mispriced by the $1.75 codex fallback
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-pro; context tier selected from billed input: <=272k short, >272k long; cached-input rate inferred as 0.1x input because the page omits it)
OpenAI docs (Codex gpt-5.3-codex; the only Codex model, flat-priced — no long-context tier)
Gemini docs (Gemini 3 Flash) — older/cheaper than the 3.5 Flash the 'flash' family now tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Pro; context tier selected from billed input: <=200k short, >200k long) — pinned because it's cheaper than the 3.x Pro the 'gemini' family tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Flash, text/image/video) — pinned because it's cheaper than the 3.5 Flash the 'flash' family tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite, text/image/video) — cheaper than 3.1 Flash-Lite the family tracks

opus-4-8.source: Anthropic docs Fast mode (Opus 4.8 $10/$50); caching multipliers apply on top of fast input

opus-4-7.source: Anthropic docs Fast mode (Opus 4.7 $30/$150). As of June 29, 2026, Fast mode is no longer available on Opus 4.6, so do not use a generic 'opus' fast family.

fable.source: Anthropic docs (Fable 5); short-alias 'fable' resolves here — full id claude-fable-5 is also pinned in models

mythos.source: Anthropic docs (Mythos 5, limited availability) — same rate card as Fable 5; short-alias 'mythos' resolves here

opus.source: Anthropic docs (Opus 4.5-4.8, flat across the full 1M window); deprecated Opus 4/4.1 ($15/$75) pinned in models

sonnet-5.source: Anthropic docs (Sonnet 5 introductory pricing through August 31, 2026); checked before generic 'sonnet' so dated build ids don't inherit Sonnet 4.5/4.6 pricing
Anthropic docs (Sonnet 5 standard pricing starting September 1, 2026)

sonnet.source: Anthropic docs (Sonnet 4.5/4.6; Sonnet 5 is pinned separately at the more specific 'sonnet-5' family)

nano.source: OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-nano; only version on the page)

mini.source: OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-mini; only version on the page, no gpt-5.5-mini exists yet)

flash-lite.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, current text/image/video); older 2.5 Flash-Lite pinned in models. Checked before 'flash'/'gemini'.

flash.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.5 Flash, current); Flash is repriced per version (3 Flash was $0.50/$3, 2.5 Flash $0.30/$2.50), so this tracks the latest and older versions are pinned in 'models'. Checked before 'gemini'.

gemini.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.1 Pro; context tier selected from billed input: <=200k short, >200k long); no per-token cache-write fee, cw=input

codex.source: fallback for unlisted codex models — gpt-5.3-codex tier through 2026-07-08
fallback for unlisted codex models — current gpt-5.6-sol flagship tier

claude.source: fallback for unknown Claude models — current Opus tier

antigravity.source: fallback — Gemini Pro tier; Google has no per-token cache-write fee, so cw=input
